lines.content: Hi, Bonnie, it's Nick with Einstein Moving Company. How you doing?
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: I'm sorry, I didn't catch that.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Yeah. Yeah, sorry, it's Nick with Einstein Moving Company. How are you doing today?
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Oh, good. I usually don't answer spam calls. So I was a little slow getting it. I got paperwork that you sent me it ended up in my spam folder so it could be till Friday to find it.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: And so I was looking over it and I called and, and spoke to somebody named Joanna, I don't know if that's in the office or.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: But anyway, I was telling on this you have let's see, sixty boxes and so I was saying, well, I told you I'd have sixty to seventy but it might be closer to eighty. And I have two pieces of furniture you know, the pipe safe and whatever and she's like, oh, well, if you're adding stuff, they'll probably have to add a third person, is that true.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: No, no, that's not. I mean, it's still the items that are in the garage, right? It's just for this move. Yeah, we can.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah. Yeah. Yeah, it's just boxes and those pieces, the furniture and then I have little odd tables, like a, excuse me, I'm choking like, little portal on top table that just got four legs and stuff like that but I was just on Google looking, do you think I need a 26 foot truck or would a twenty foot be big enough?
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: So, yeah, so we only, we only have 26 foot trucks. So that would be just, yeah, the size of the vehicle that we come out and perform me move-in but yeah, I, it's not like you, I need, it's not like I think you're gonna fill up the entire truck or anything like that but yeah, and I don't, I don't think we are going to have to add a third person either. I think Joanna just wasn't really privy to, you know, the information that you shared with me about the move. So I think we're set up for a success with what we have now.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah, and it wasn't on here. So I thought maybe she's looking at this and see sixty boxes and everything else I have to pay for, so, pay extra. So it's mostly just boxes of Christmas items. Scrapbooks nothing is exceptionally heavy except deposit. But I mean, all the boxes I'm 74 and I left them all over the place and up in the attic and whatever, so I need to get the ones down out of the attic but I don't want to get them until like next week. So total, I'll probably have $70 plus boxes. I've emptied some of them and hauled, it's a goodwill. I still am gonna do that this week but the little stuff, you know, and, and the furniture that's out there, I would like to get it out so I can get the garage painted.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: So, okay, well, she kinda scared me. It's like, oh, no, are you gonna start adding everything? I say you're going to be less another $20.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: But I'm no, no, I just figured she was answering service didn't seem to actually know anyway, cause I was looking up. Did I really need a truck that, that's that big but I really don't, but if that's all you have, I guess that, that's what I get.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: No, no. I'm sorry about that.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah, yeah, it's not gonna be a problem. You know, always good to have more space to play around with. Cause that just means the load it will take less time cause they're not having to place so much Tores.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah. Well, all these boxes are the same size so they stack on each other cause I went out and measured them. They're like, you know, those plastic 64 court or whatever they are. They're like eighteen by sixteen by thirteen so you won't be playing Teri. I just have a few, probably not even five or six that are in cardboard boxes cause I don't like cardboard. And so, that's what I was thinking these little tables and things I need to get out of the house because I was told it's too crowded.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: And so I talked to the lady that has the shop it's across the street from where my son lives and so he will be there on Saturday to help you all, you know, next week to help you unload and whatever. So, I didn't really see a, a need for an extra person, but I just wanted to confirm that with you.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Sure. Yeah, I don't, I don't think that'll be necessary. We can handle this move with two people.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah, and so anyway, I will fax you or I may just send it from my phone. It's easier for me. I'm not all tech savvy but I'll just find the email in my phone and text you pictures of of what the garage looks like right now. But I mean even if I get the ones down out of the attic it's not gonna be like a half a garage full anyway. So that's why I was thinking the little stuff that I don't really need here. I can just throw in the back, you know a little quilt.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Okay, okay, well I feel better now.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Good, alright. I'm glad I so sorry I was out of out of the office. So we couldn't you know, go over this stuff a little sooner but yeah, we should be all, all set on on our end and we're good for the eleventh and also just let me know when you want to set up the rest of the move and I'd be happy to help you out with that. Okay.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: You mean the full move. I got to sell my house first but this one.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Yeah, yeah, when, whenever that is we'd love to help out with that one.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah. And I probably would need a the 26 foot cause I have furniture but I'm going to get rid of my sofa and mattress, and, you know, stuff like that I don't really need, or that I need new when I get down there.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: There anyway, so another 26 foot would probably be good but I would like to get rid of some little stuff now and you know, maybe some more boxes. But this would be at my son's mother-in-law. And they live right across the street. So before you go or when you all are here I guess I'll get your number so I can send to him, so he's not getting a spam call cause he's probably like me he won't answer. So if you need anything, any extra directions or whatever and then I'll give you.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: So it'll, it'll actually be the movers of their personal phone numbers that they'll get in contact with either day of the move. This is our office line. So yeah, it'll be, it'll be them you know contacting you the morning of the move.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: So it won't be you, it'll be somebody else.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Correct, yeah, it'll be, it'll be some movers that we've got and you'll actually receive a meet your movers email probably three days leading up to the move and it's pretty, pretty cool feature you're able to go online and match the pictures and we have profiles of our movers as well so you can kind of get to know them a little bit before they start moving your stuff. Yup.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Yeah, cause that's what I thought it would be your personal phone number. So I'll give you that or whoever the mover shows up and then I'll give my son their numbers. So, if they try to call him, you know, he won't just ignore it like I do cause I get tons of spam calls everyday. I almost didn't answer this one. But anyway, yeah, so anyway, we should be good to go. Cause.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: I may add a few more boxes. I want to take some stuff out of the tops of closet and then this is the big shock. It used to be like a sheet metal shop. So there's plenty of room to back into this little road and you know, dump everything there but like I said, he will be there to help you and show you where everything needs to go. So, I think we should be good to go. This will take a little off of well I appreciate it and thank you for calling me back and.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Yeah, of course, we need to.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: So I just go ahead and respond to this email and send you pictures. So you have some idea, is that what you wanted me to do or does it even matter?
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Yes ma'am. Yeah, yeah, that'll, that'll work and just there's, there's no huge rush on that either, okay.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel

lines.content: Okay. Alright that sounds good. Then. I will do that. I'll finish putting my boxes out there and then send you some photos.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Okay, perfect. Thank you so much.
lines.name: Bonita Triplett

lines.content: Yes, ma'am, you have a good day. Bye-bye.
lines.name: Nicholas Dagel
